Research Domain

Understanding the Problem Space

01

Literature Survey

Sri Lanka's welfare programs — Samurdhi, disability allowances, elderly pensions, and nutritional aid — suffer from manual processing, fragmented data, and delayed approvals. Global e-governance successes in India and Bangladesh highlight the urgent need for a digitized, unified system in Sri Lanka.

Ahmed, M.U. (2019) UN E-Gov Survey (2022) ICTA SL (2023) Bhatnagar (2004)
02

Research Gap

  • No unified digital platform for welfare management in Sri Lanka
  • Absence of AI-driven eligibility assessment tools
  • No personalized healthcare recommendation integrated into welfare systems
  • Lack of multilingual citizen support systems (Sinhala/Tamil)
  • No GIS-based visualization of vulnerable populations
  • No integrated financial guidance for welfare recipients
03

Research Problem

How can a centralized, AI-integrated digital platform improve the transparency, efficiency, and accessibility of Sri Lanka's social welfare services — ensuring aid reaches the most vulnerable citizens through data-driven, personalized, and geographically-aware decision-making?

04

Main Objective

To design and develop an AI-integrated digital welfare platform to improve efficiency, transparency, and accessibility in Sri Lanka's social protection programs.

AI Eligibility Prediction & Healthcare Recommendation
Multilingual Chatbot (Sinhala / Tamil / English)
AI-based Financial Guidance Module
AI + GIS Mapping for Vulnerable Populations
05

Methodology

📦
Data Collection
Socio-economic, health & geographic datasets
→
🧠
Model Training
Decision Tree, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, Gradient Boosting
→
🛠️
Platform Dev
Streamlit web app with 3-tier architecture
→
🔗
Integration
AI modules + GIS + Chatbot + Financial guidance unified
→
✅
Evaluation
UAT, accuracy metrics, performance testing
06

Project Timeline

Milestones & Assessments

✅ Completed

Topic Assessment Form (TAF)

📅 June 2025📋 Project ID: 25-26J-320

Research topic approval covering problem statement, objectives, novelty verification, specialization alignment, and supervisor sign-off by Sr. Lecturer Anjalie Gamage.

✅ Completed

Project Proposal

📅 September 2025📝 Document + Presentation

Detailed project scope, literature review, methodology, system architecture design, individual component specifications, and project timeline submission.

✅ Completed

Progress Presentation 1 (PP1)

📅 January 2026🎤 Presentation + Demo

Initial prototype demonstration, dataset construction results, early ML model training outcomes, and checklist submission confirming project progress.

✅ Completed

Progress Presentation 2 (PP2)

📅 April 2026🎤 Presentation + System Demo

Working AI modules demonstration including Gradient Boosting model, healthcare recommendation engine, GIS mapping, and multilingual chatbot integration.

✅ Completed

Final Report Submission

📅 May 2026📄 4 Individual + 1 Group Report

Complete individual final reports submitted by all team members with full system documentation, research findings, model evaluations, and future enhancement roadmaps.

🎓 Final Step

Final Assessment & Viva Voce

📅 May 2026🏆 Oral Defense

Complete system demonstration and individual oral examination defending the research methodology, AI model outcomes, and overall project contributions before the evaluation panel.

The Team

Meet the Researchers

A multidisciplinary team from SLIIT, each specializing in a distinct AI domain to build an impactful welfare platform for Sri Lanka.

ML / Health AI

Manawadu G.M.S.T

IT22926494
AI Eligibility Prediction & Healthcare Recommendation

Develops the ML-based eligibility prediction engine using Gradient Boosting (91.8% accuracy on 1,830 records) and healthcare recommendation system mapping hospitals and specialists across all 25 Sri Lanka districts.

Machine LearningHealth InformaticsPython / scikit-learn
✉ it22926494@my.sliit.lk
NLP / Conv AI

Shamika A.G

IT22585080
Multilingual Chatbot Development

Builds the first-ever tri-lingual conversational assistant for Sri Lankan welfare services supporting Sinhala, Tamil, and English using Rasa and spaCy for intent recognition and dialogue management.

NLPRasa / spaCyConversational AI
✉ it22585080@my.sliit.lk
FinTech AI

Dissanayake D.M.V.S

IT22366818
AI-based Financial Guidance Module

Creates the smart investment plan recommendation system for low-income welfare recipients using clustering and collaborative filtering to suggest safe micro-investment and financial aid schemes.

Financial AIRecommender SystemsClustering
✉ it22366818@my.sliit.lk
GeoSpatial AI

Wickramasinghe R.P.Y.N

IT22214584
AI + GIS Mapping for Vulnerable Groups

Leads the predictive geospatial AI system using spatial data analysis and heatmap visualization to identify regions with high concentrations of disabled, pregnant, or chronically ill populations for policy-level insights.

Geospatial AIGIS / LeafletPredictive Analytics
✉ it22214584@my.sliit.lk
